reasoning: As of early November 2025, the price of gold is trading around $4,000 per ounce, having recently reached all-time highs near $4,400 in mid-October. For the COMEX Gold Continuous Contract to close below $3,200 by the end of 2025, it would require an additional drop of 20% in less than two months. While strong pullbacks in gold can happen, a crash of this magnitude within such a short timeframe is historically very rare (only a small single-digit probability based on historical distributions of 2-month gold returns). The Polymarket consensus was already 93% on October 30, and there is no evidence suggesting a sudden collapse in gold prices that would make the $3,200 level realistically at risk by year-end. Taking all factors into account, the likelihood of gold closing at or above $3,200 is extremely high.
